# SaaS Contracts Guide 2026 - Andrew S. Bosin LLC (SaaS Law Firm)

## Summary

Practitioner attorney guide covering the full SaaS legal contract stack from a law-firm perspective. Published March 2026. Covers when to upgrade contracts, common mistakes, and 2026-specific additions (AI compliance clauses). High authority: SaaS-specialized attorney.

## Key Facts

### How SaaS Contracts Work Together

The complete SaaS legal stack:
- **ToS** - governs all users
- **Privacy Policy** - governs data practices
- **Subscription Agreement** - governs paying customers
- **SLA + DPA** - layered on for compliance and performance
- **MSA** - used for enterprise deals

### When to Upgrade Contracts

Triggers for contract revision:
- You start charging customers
- You expand internationally
- You close enterprise deals
- You raise venture funding
- You handle sensitive data

### 2026-Specific: AI Compliance Clauses

> "AI-specific clauses are becoming increasingly important in 2026." Modern SaaS agreements must address AI compliance, including data use and liability provisions.

This is a new required consideration not present in most legacy ToS templates.

### Fixed-Fee vs Custom Contract Packages

Many startups use fixed-fee legal packages that typically include:
- Terms of Service
- Privacy Policy
- Basic SLA
- DPA template

More advanced agreements (MSAs) are usually custom-priced.

### Common SaaS Contract Mistakes

- Copying generic templates from the internet
- Failing to include proper liability protections
- Ignoring data privacy compliance
- Not updating contracts as the company scales
- Using overly complex legal language that scares customers

## Key Quotations

- "Often overlaps with Terms of Service but is more customized. Modern agreements must address AI compliance, including data use and liability provisions."
- "You should revisit your SaaS agreements when: You start charging customers / You expand internationally / You close enterprise deals / You raise venture funding / You handle sensitive data."
